title: Output Filtering
concept: Scanning agent outputs for leaked API keys, database credentials, or inappropriate language before display.

exercises.prompt: What do we check before allowing AI actions to happen?
exercises.explain: We run proposed actions against regex or policy rules to ensure they are safe before executing them.

cards.title: What is Output Filtering?
cards.body: Output filtering is the process of checking what an AI produces to make sure it's safe and appropriate.

cards.title: Definition of Output Filtering
cards.body: It is the method of scanning AI-generated texts to remove sensitive information, like API keys and to check for inappropriate language.

cards.title: Analogy: The Security Guard
cards.body: Think of output filtering like a security guard who checks bags at the entrance of a concert, ensuring no harmful items get inside.

cards.title: Example of Output Filtering
cards.body: Before showing an AI’s response to a user, we check if it contains any exposed database credentials or bad language.

cards.title: Myth: Output Filtering is Not Needed
cards.body: Some may think output filtering is unnecessary, but it is crucial to prevent sensitive information leaks and ensure user safety.

cards.title: Visual: Safe vs. Unsafe Outputs
cards.body: Filtering helps us choose the right outputs to show users.

right.text: This is a safe and clean response.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of output filtering?

options.text: To ensure the AI does not leak sensitive data

exercises.explain: The main goal of output filtering is to protect sensitive data and ensure responses are safe for users.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Output filtering checks can help prevent the display of inappropriate language.
exercises.explain: True, output filtering is designed to catch inappropriate language before it reaches the user.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Output filtering helps to scan for __________ before displaying AI-generated text.
exercises.sentence: Output filtering helps to scan for sensitive information before displaying AI-generated text.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'sensitive information', which includes API keys and credentials.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of output filtering?

options.text: Displaying all AI responses directly to users

exercises.explain: Option 2 is correct because it describes filtering responses to check for inappropriate language before showing them.

exercises.prompt: What should a good output filtering process achieve?

options.text: Cleaning AI outputs for safety and appropriateness
Making AI outputs faster to generate
Limiting the number of user questions

exercises.explain: A good output filtering process ensures that all AI outputs are cleaned for safety and appropriateness, removing any sensitive information or inappropriate language before display.
